resources.string: Press the button below to get an AI-powered summary of the content
Embeddings will be recalculated after strategy change
No new articles for today’s summary.
Local model is not available. Comparison cannot be performed without it.
All available AI models failed to respond. Try again later.
Local summarization does not support Q&A
Only extractive if article is shorter than: %d
Compress more aggressively if article is longer than: %d
Chars per article answer in feed: %d
Delete all feed articles? Sources will stay.
Delete all embeddings? This will also clear similarity cache.
Required to encrypt API keys for both synchronization (Firebase) and backups (import/export).
This passphrase is used to encrypt API keys during export and cloud sync. On a new device, you need to enter it again to unlock the keys.
Passphrase must be at least 8 characters long
Here are the top news for this period:
No news found. Your filters or settings may be too strict, or there may be no sources.
Unable to summarize the feed or answer questions about it because the feed is empty.
Use full texts instead of excerpts for feed summaries
No summaries found. Your filters or settings may be too strict, or scheduled summaries are not enabled.
No summaries found. Your filters or settings may be too strict, or scheduled summaries are not enabled.
Although the news items cover the same topics or events, no shared fragments could be identified beyond their headlines.
No unique fragments were detected. The news may be heavily rephrasing one another, very similar, or too short.
These news items cover the same topic, but no meaningful common fragments were found beyond their headlines.
There are no sources in this group

— This section contains account sign-in, synchronization, and data transfer controls.
— In the sync block you choose strategy, overwrite priority, interval, and included data.
— In the import block you separately choose strategy: merge or overwrite local data.
— These choices define which side becomes the source of truth on conflicts between local, cloud, and backup data.

— This section contains AI strategy, limits, local summary, deduplication, and adaptive logic.\n— It directly affects quality, speed, and cost of AI processing.\n— Inside, each block opens its own short explanation for its settings.
— This section manages keys and models for summary and embedding tasks.\n— It defines which AI configurations are available to the app.\n— Inside, each block separately explains its models and keys.
— This section controls recommendations and topic subscriptions.\n— It affects source suggestion blocks and quick theme subscriptions.\n— Inside, each settings block has its own help target.

— This section controls feed layout, filtering, deduplication, and the local model.\n— It defines how compact or detailed the reading experience feels.\n— Inside, each block explains its specific switches, thresholds, and actions.
— This section contains schedule, push notifications, and summary history parameters.\n— It controls automatic daily summary generation.\n— Inside, each block has its own short settings explanation.
— This section controls app language, summary language, and theme.\n— It defines the global interface behavior across the app.\n— Inside, help opens separately for each settings block.

— This section contains auto-cleanup, local data cleanup, and reset actions.\n— It affects stored articles, embeddings, and summary history.\n— Inside, each block separately explains what will be changed or removed.

— This button opens the AI dialog for the current filtered feed.\n— Only the news currently visible on screen is included in the summary.\n— Inside the dialog you can regenerate, ask follow-up questions, or jump to sources.

— Search filters the feed by titles and text.
— Date limits the time range of shown news.
— Saved shows only bookmarked items.
— Group narrows the feed to selected sources.
— PDF exports exactly the currently filtered list.
— This area shows the feed processing state.\n— The app is grouping similar news and removing duplicates.\n— When processing finishes, the final card list appears here.
— An empty feed means no news matches the current filters.\n— The reason may be date, group, or saved-only mode.\n— Try refreshing sources or relaxing the filters.
— This is the basic news or cluster card in the feed.\n— From it you can open the source, save the item, or view media.\n— Buttons inside the card handle AI actions and duplicate-related actions.
— This button creates a new source folder.\n— Groups help organize subscriptions by topic or use case.\n— After creation you add concrete RSS, Telegram, or YouTube sources into it.
— This is a source group card, or folder.\n— Inside it you can enable or disable the whole group or individual sources.\n— A disabled group does not participate in feed generation.
— This area shows ready-made topic subscriptions.\n— It helps add sets of sources without manual search.\n— After subscribing, themes become separate structured groups.
— This block shows the scheduled summary state.\n— It displays the previous and next summary generation times.\n— It is a quick indicator of when a summary was created or is expected next.
— This area shows the current main summary.\n— The cards below group key themes and stories.\n— From here you can jump to related sources and content.
— The infographic shows the summary in a compact analytical view.\n— It displays key stories and aggregated metrics.\n— Tapping items opens related content or sources.
— This button opens the history of previously generated summaries.\n— It lets you return to older digests.\n— In history mode you get search, filters, selection, and deletion actions.

— Search finds the summaries you need by text inside the summary content.
— The date filter limits the history to a selected time range.
— The saved filter shows only favorited summaries.
— PDF export saves exactly the currently filtered summary history.

— A card shows a short preview of a previously generated summary.
— A normal tap opens the full summary content.
— A long press enables selection mode for bulk actions.
— The footer actions let you favorite or delete the summary.

— This block shows the current account state: signed in or signed out.
— It displays the active profile name and email.
— The sign-in button opens authentication, while sign-out ends the current session.
— If no account is connected, cloud sync cannot work fully.

— Sync strategy selects the mode: Overwrite or Merge.
— Overwrite priority is used in Overwrite mode: Local (local → cloud) or Cloud (cloud → local if cloud is newer).
— Auto-sync interval controls background sync frequency.
— Backup checkboxes define which categories are included in sync/backup.
— API keys are synced only when that option is enabled and an encryption passphrase is set.
— “Sync now” starts a manual sync, and the line below shows the timestamp of the last successful sync.

— This block controls which data goes into the backup file during export.
— It does not affect automatic cloud synchronization.
— API keys can be exported only after you configure an encryption passphrase.
— The export button creates a file containing only the selected categories.

— Import strategy defines the mode: Merge (add to local data) or Overwrite (replace local data with file data).
— The checkboxes below define which categories will be read from the backup file.
— This does not affect auto-sync and does not change export contents.
— API keys are imported only when the option is enabled and the encryption passphrase is configured.
— The import button applies only selected categories using the chosen strategy.

— This is a separate passphrase used to encrypt API keys in backups and Firebase sync.
— The keys themselves are never uploaded to the cloud in plain text.
— On a new device, you need to enter this passphrase again or the API keys will stay locked.
— Clearing the passphrase also disables API-key sync.
— App language changes the UI localization.\n— It affects menus, buttons, dialogs, and help texts.\n— The change applies across the whole app, not only to the current group.

— Summary language defines which language AI should use for generated summaries.
— Ukrainian forces responses in Ukrainian regardless of the source language.
— English forces responses in English.
— This setting affects summaries, AI answers in the feed, and related summary flows.
— Theme controls how the app looks visually.\n— System follows the device theme.\n— Light and Dark force the chosen appearance regardless of the system setting.

— This block stores keys and models used for summary generation.
— You can add multiple configurations and keep only the needed ones enabled.
— Editing changes the model, display name, or access key.
— Disabled configurations are ignored during AI requests.

— This block manages models used for embeddings and similarity-related tasks.\n— These configurations are used for vectorization and parts of deduplication.\n— You can add, edit, disable, or delete individual models.

— Local strategy tries to work without cloud models.\n— Cloud strategy relies on external AI providers.\n— Adaptive strategy switches between local and cloud processing depending on limits and runtime conditions.

— Characters per article while processing limits how much text from one article is analyzed.
— Characters per feed article limits the input size in feed-specific AI scenarios.
— Total characters limits the full payload size for a single AI request.
— Lower values speed requests up, but they may cut useful context.

— Feed news count for extractive summarization defines how many feed items local summary will process.\n— Scheduled summary news count for extractive summarization defines the same limit for scheduled summaries.\n— These settings mainly affect local and fallback flows.

— Local deduplication compares news without a cloud call.\n— Cloud deduplication relies on external AI processing.\n— Adaptive deduplication switches between those approaches depending on available context and runtime conditions.

— The switch enables your own prompt instead of the default template.\n— The text field lets you refine the style or structure of the summary.\n— A custom prompt is an extra instruction, but it does not replace the technical output format rules.

— If an article is shorter than the lower threshold, adaptive mode finishes with pure extractive summarization.
— If an article is longer than the lower threshold but shorter than the upper one, the text is compressed before cloud processing to the medium percentage.
— If an article is longer than the upper threshold, the text is compressed more aggressively using the long-article percentage.
— Together these settings control when adaptive mode switches to cloud and how aggressively it compresses the text first.

— Show media enables images and previews in feed cards.
— Show description adds a text preview below the headline.
— Use full text for feed summary expands the context available for AI summarization.
— Informativeness filters out less useful material.
— Deduplication removes similar news from the feed.
— Hide single items removes entries that did not gather enough matches.
— Local and cloud similarity thresholds define how close items must be to be merged.
— Minimum mentions defines how many matches are needed before a topic is treated as a group.
— The model status block and button below control downloading or deleting the local model.

— Scheduled summary enables automatic digest generation by schedule.
— Push notifications control notifications after a summary is created.
— Time defines when the daily summary runs.
— Show latest summaries sets the history size on the summary screen.
— Show news in infographic defines how many items appear in the summary dashboard infographic.
— Show recommendations enables topic suggestions and quick subscription blocks.\n— It controls whether recommended themes and guided source suggestions appear in the app.

— Article auto cleanup defines after how many days old articles are removed automatically.
— Clear articles removes the local feed content but keeps the sources.
— Clear embeddings resets vector data and similarity cache.
— Clear scheduled summaries removes stored scheduled summary history.
— Reset settings returns app preferences to their default values.
Task: group the material into key themes, keep the most important stories, write short headlines, and attach each story to the best source.
Your sources, subscriptions, and API keys\nwill be available on all devices
A group with this name already exists
This folder name matches an existing subscription
A source with this name already exists
A source with this URL already exists
A config with this name already exists
API key must not contain spaces
This API key is already added
Delete group \"%s\" and all its sources?
Delete selected groups (%d) and all their sources?
